_comment: handoff-fanout config (referenced from $HANDOFF_HOME/config.json). Drop unused keys; all fields are optional with safe defaults. A SINGLE $HANDOFF_HOME/config.json is SHARED by every project under that home — so anything PROJECT-SPECIFIC (a project's red lines, its roadmap, a repo-bound baseline hook) MUST be project-scoped (see project_inject_blocks / the per-hook+roadmap `projects` field) or it leaks into every sibling project's agent session.
_inject_blocks_comment: TRULY GLOBAL blocks injected into EVERY project's handoff prompt. Put project-specific red lines in project_inject_blocks, NOT here.
inject_blocks:
## Global red line (example, applies to all projects)
- never force-push a shared branch without the owner's go-ahead
_project_inject_blocks_comment: Per-project inject blocks: {slug: [block, ...]}. Injected ONLY into that project's prompts (additive to the global inject_blocks). This is where a project's OWN red lines belong so they never leak into siblings.

my-repo:
## my-repo red lines (example, project-scoped)
- never edit src/legacy/ without an ADR
- treasury operations must go through journal_service.post_entry()

baseline_hooks._comment: Each hook runs in the workspace dir; the captured output is added to the handoff baseline block. `regex` is optional — when set, the first match group is stored. `projects` scopes the hook: EMPTY/absent = all projects; a non-empty list runs it ONLY for those projects (a repo-bound command like `alembic current` belongs scoped so it never runs in a sibling's workspace).
baseline_hooks.name: Alembic

baseline_hooks._comment: A generic, project-agnostic hook leaves `projects` absent so it applies everywhere.
baseline_hooks.name: PythonVersion

roadmap._comment: Optional file whose phase sections are excerpted into the handoff prompt. `projects` scopes it: EMPTY/absent = all projects; a non-empty list excerpts it ONLY into those projects' prompts (a project's own roadmap must be scoped so it does not bleed into siblings).

_singlepane_projects_comment: Projects whose cold-spawned VS Code window should open as a single editor pane (no side bars) WITHOUT git-worktree isolation. The watchdog generates an out-of-tree .handoff.code-workspace (folder = the real project dir, window.title carries the task) and opens it; the handoff-helper extension collapses the side bars on load (it guards on the .handoff.code-workspace suffix). EMPTY/absent = no project opts in (legacy / byte-identical).
_mandate_projects_comment: Optional v5.4 mandate roll-out allowlist (must be a NON-EMPTY list of slugs). The global dump entry re-execs the engine, so the global HANDOFF_RETRO_MANDATE / HANDOFF_AUDIT_MANDATE env would enforce on EVERY project's active dump. List only projects ready for the precheck + --retro-evidence flow; unlisted siblings take the legacy (no-gate) path. FAIL-CLOSED: every degenerate shape — key absent, [], a bare string typo, all-invalid entries — enforces EVERYWHERE (an accidental empty never silently disables the mandate). To genuinely disable, unset the env mandate, not this key. An explicit --retro-evidence or HANDOFF_RETRO_BYPASS always runs the gate regardless.
